message:
🌱 falco: [Tracking] Opening multiple event sources in the same Falco instance (#1947)

* 🌱 Add falco: [Tracking] Opening multiple event sources in the same Falco mission

* 🌱 Add falco: [Tracking] Opening multiple event sources in the same Falco mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct falco multi-source fix PR and replace raw content

Changed fix PR from #2080 (CLOSED, never merged - was a rate limiter cleanup)
to #2182 (MERGED - the actual multi-threaded parallel event source implementation).
Replaced resolution text with accurate description of multi-source support
added in Falco 0.33.0. Added proper configuration and verification steps.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "falco-2074-tracking-opening-multiple-event-sources-in-the-same-falco-instance",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "falco: [Tracking] Opening multiple event sources in the same Falco instance",
+    "description": "Support running multiple event sources (syscall, k8s_audit, plugins) in parallel within a single Falco instance. Previously Falco could only open one event source at a time, requiring multiple Falco instances for different sources. Fixed in Falco 0.33.0 via a multi-threaded architecture redesign (PR #2182).",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Verify Falco version supports multiple sources",
+        "description": "Multi-source support was added in Falco 0.33.0. Check your version:\n\nUpgrade to Falco 0.33.0+ if running an older version."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure multiple event sources",
+        "description": "Enable multiple sources in your Falco configuration (falco.yaml):\n\nThis configures Falco to use the modern BPF engine for syscalls while simultaneously processing K8s audit events via the plugin."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify multiple sources are active",
+        "description": "Check Falco logs to confirm multiple sources are running in parallel:\n\nYou should see log entries for each active source (e.g., \"syscall\" and \"k8s_audit\") running concurrently."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Falco 0.33.0+ supports running multiple event sources in parallel within a single instance using a multi-threaded architecture. Each source runs in its own thread with independent rule processing. This was implemented in PR #2182, which redesigned the event processing engine to support concurrent event sources.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "falco --option engine.kind=modern_bpf -o \"plugins[].name=k8saudit\" -o \"plugins[].library_path=/usr/share/falco/plugins/libk8saudit.so\""
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "falco",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "falco"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco/issues/2074",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco/pull/2182"
+    },
+    "reactions": 10,
+    "comments": 19,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-03T06:32:57.538Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
